message:
🌱 Add beads restore to all AGENT_LOOP_PROMPTs — both systemd and manual restarts now read beads

All 5 agents (scanner, reviewer, feature, outreach, supervisor) now run
bd list + bd ready on every startup regardless of restart path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-
 # /loop cadence is 30 minutes — this is slow thoughtful work, not reactive triage.
 # The real policy is in project_feature_policy.md; re-read every iteration at Step 0.
 # First-task seed: start with kubestellar/console#8695 (responsive-layout RFC).
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="Register: /loop 30m Follow every rule in project_feature_policy.md from memory (lane boundary, RFC structure, GA4 signal proposals, help-wanted grooming, phase-handoff to scanner). At the START of each iteration print FEATURE_START_ET=\$(TZ=America/New_York date \"+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z\") and NEXT_FEATURE_ET = the next */30 firing expressed in America/New_York. At the END print FEATURE_END_ET and restate NEXT_FEATURE_ET. All timestamps MUST be in Eastern Time (America/New_York), never UTC. AFTER registering the cron, immediately run ONE kickoff iteration right now focused on kubestellar/console#8695 per the 'First task' section of the policy — produce the RFC, publish it as a comment on the GitHub issue, and file scanner-handoff beads for each phase. Do NOT wait for the first 30m firing."
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Register: /loop 30m Follow every rule in project_feature_policy.md from memory (lane boundary, RFC structure, GA4 signal proposals, help-wanted grooming, phase-handoff to scanner). At the START of each iteration print FEATURE_START_ET=$(TZ=America/New_York date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z") and NEXT_FEATURE_ET = the next */30 firing expressed in America/New_York. At the END print FEATURE_END_ET and restate NEXT_FEATURE_ET. All timestamps MUST be in Eastern Time (America/New_York), never UTC. AFTER registering the cron, immediately run ONE kickoff iteration right now focused on kubestellar/console#8695 per the 'First task' section of the policy — produce the RFC, publish it as a comment on the GitHub issue, and file scanner-handoff beads for each phase. Do NOT wait for the first 30m firing."
 
 AGENT_LOG_FILE=/home/dev/.claude/projects/-Users-andan02/memory/feature_log.md

@@ -1 +1 @@
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="Re-read project_outreach_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no /loop, no cron, no self-scheduling. Stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. LANE — you own ONLY: awesome lists, directories, comparison sites, aggregators, community forums, package registries, CNCF landscape entries, and any public index where KubeStellar Console should be listed. Target 200+ awesome-list placements. LANE BOUNDARIES — never touch GitHub issues or PRs in any kubestellar repo, never fix bugs, review code, implement features, or do anything the scanner/reviewer/architect agents do. If you discover a bug or improvement, file a beads issue for the scanner — do not act on it yourself. Fork under clubanderson for all external PRs. If no directive arrives within 2h, run one proactive awesome-list scan, log to outreach_log.md, then return to idle."
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Re-read project_outreach_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no /loop, no cron, no self-scheduling. Stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. LANE — you own ONLY: awesome lists, directories, comparison sites, aggregators, community forums, package registries, CNCF landscape entries, and any public index where KubeStellar Console should be listed. Target 200+ awesome-list placements. LANE BOUNDARIES — never touch GitHub issues or PRs in any kubestellar repo, never fix bugs, review code, implement features, or do anything the scanner/reviewer/architect agents do. If you discover a bug or improvement, file a beads issue for the scanner — do not act on it yourself. Fork under clubanderson for all external PRs. If no directive arrives within 2h, run one proactive awesome-list scan, log to outreach_log.md, then return to idle."

@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-
 
 # Short loop prompt — the real policy is in project_reviewer_policy.md, re-read
 # every iteration at Step 0.
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="Re-read project_reviewer_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no self-scheduling, no /loop cron. Then stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. If no directive arrives within 45 min, run one proactive nightly regression + CodeQL drain pass, log it to reviewer_log.md, then return to idle."
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Re-read project_reviewer_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no self-scheduling, no /loop cron. Then stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. If no directive arrives within 45 min, run one proactive nightly regression + CodeQL drain pass, log it to reviewer_log.md, then return to idle."
 
 AGENT_LOG_FILE=/home/dev/.claude/projects/-Users-andan02/memory/reviewer_log.md

@@ -4,3 +4,5 @@
 # This file is sourced by the claude-dev local script when --copilot/--claude
 # is passed to the scanner.
 AGENT_CLI=copilot
+
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="You are the KubeStellar Scanner in EXECUTOR MODE — no self-scheduling. On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor."

@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ AGENT_SESSION_NAME=supervisor
 AGENT_WORKDIR=/home/dev/kubestellar-console
 AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-opus-4-7"
 
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="You are the Supervisor. \
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. You are the Supervisor. \
 \
 FIRST — read the kubestellar/supervised-agent repo entirely for full runtime context: \
 read all files under bin/, config/, docs/, examples/ in /tmp/supervised-agent/ (git pull first). \
